name: MDX (Markdown + JSX)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Unified Collective / Josh Comeau ecosystem

performance.notes: Build-time compilation; runtime rendering is fast

agent_readiness.notes: Write JSX in Markdown files. Import and use React components inside .mdx files. Used by Next.js, Gatsby, Astro, Docusaurus, Nextra. compile(source) → JavaScript module. Plugins via unified remark/rehype ecosystem. Frontmatter support via remark-frontmatter. Syntax highlighting via rehype-pretty-code or rehype-highlight. @next/mdx for Next.js. MDXRemote for runtime compilation. v3 uses ESM. Standard for documentation sites, blogs, and component libraries wanting rich content.

gotchas: JSX in Markdown can be confusing for non-React devs
XSS risk if rendering untrusted MDX content
v2 vs v3 migration required for ESM changes
best_when: Documentation sites needing interactive React components in content
Blogs where code examples need live component previews
avoid_when: Non-React/JSX stacks (use Astro components or plain Markdown)
Untrusted user-generated content (XSS risk without sandboxing)
Simple documentation without interactive components (plain Markdown sufficient)
summary: MDX lets you write JSX in Markdown files — import and use React components inside .mdx content. Used by Next.js, Gatsby, Astro, Docusaurus. compile() API, unified plugin ecosystem, frontmatter support. v3 ESM-native. Standard for documentation sites, component library docs, and interactive blogs where code demos need live React components alongside Markdown prose.
